User client could silently disconnect (network issues, server restart),
stopping all channel parsing while the bot part kept working normally.
Added a 60s health check loop that detects disconnection and auto-reconnects.

so pytest can find and run tests in the Docker container.

commit.message:
Unstable Devices activation-time trigger + Power Converter combat-time trigger

Unstable Devices (Saska Teft): moved from incorrect round-start to activation-time.
Free once-per-activation button in thread; auto-confirm if 1 friendly in LOS,
picker if multiple. Grants 1 Device token to chosen friendly.

Power Converter (Saska Teft): moved from activation-start auto-discard to
combat-time hand-channel prompt. When a friendly with a Device token attacks,
pings Saska's player in hand channel to approve. On approve: die picker →
optional color swap → reroll. Once per round. Device token NOT consumed.

commit.message:
refactor: rewrite SQL Patterns — 3 role-based pattern cards (10 → 3)

1. Backend (Application Queries): CRUD, JOIN fetch, pagination, EXISTS validation, transactions
2. Data Engineer (Data Processing): transformation (CASE/CAST/DATE_TRUNC), aggregation, pipeline JOIN, window functions, deduplication
3. Data Analyst (Product Analytics): DAU, Day-1 retention, funnel analysis, top users, sessions per day

Each card has a full mindmap tree in the tip block.

commit.message:
Force cache refresh when sessions are in ambiguous status (#335)

When a live session has no idle signal but was classified as fresh/typing
(transcriptContains didn't find the user entry yet), don't store the
fingerprint. This forces re-evaluation on the next poll cycle instead of
caching the stale status for up to MAX_FINGERPRINT_AGE (30s).

Fixes the same class of bug as #330 but for non-pool sessions (external,
custom, sub-claude) where the pool-slot shortcut doesn't apply.

Co-authored-by: EliasSchlie <EliasSchlie@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle mono Link Audio sources by duplicating to stereo

USoundWaveProcedural is 2ch fixed; queuing 1ch data into it breaks
the mixer (silent output). Convert mono to stereo by duplicating
each sample to L and R at unity gain before QueueAudio, matching
standard DAW bus routing convention.

commit.message:
fix: resolve ragdoll CG solver stability — eliminate energy injection

Multiple serious stability issues in the CG/TGS coupled solver for
TIER_ANIM ragdoll physics have been identified and fixed:

- Contact constraints now linear-only at build time: zero J_wa/J_wb
  angular lever-arm Jacobians on all contact rows. The cross-product
  terms (rA×n, rB×n) were injecting angular energy that fought joint
  constraints and caused ragdoll explosion on ground contact.

- Velocity damping applied per CG iteration (not once post-solve),
  damping only the solver delta — preserves gravity/external forces.

- Gyroscopic torque correction moved outside the CG iteration loop;
  was running 16x per tick with full dt, massively over-correcting.

- FK position projection (propagate_coupled_anchors_) removed from
  the CG iteration loop — it teleported child body positions to
  enforce anchor coincidence without velocity correction, injecting
  kinetic energy.

- Final coupled_position_projection_ disabled for same reason.

- Baumgarte bias reduced to 0.01 for CG contact rebuilds — the full
  0.1 was re-applied every iteration, pumping energy through contacts.

- TIER_ANIM routed through TGS (CG coupled solver) consistently in
  both tier_cross.c and step_plan.c.

Result: ragdoll drops from height, lands, and settles stably at
avg Y = -0.15 (near ground). Angular constraints that exist are
enforced; rig needs more angular constraints for visual consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  and SiteURL signals in the support package

  openldap, keycloak, minio, mattermost) in kubernetes/ subdirectory

Fix silent email failures: check Resend v3 error response

resend.emails.send() in v3 returns {data, error} instead of throwing.
Previous code ignored the error entirely, so failed sends (wrong domain,
invalid key, quota) appeared successful. Now throws so callers surface
the real failure in logs.

- CONDENSER: Nusselt film condensation (existing)

- LIQUID_LIQUID: Kern shell-side cross-flow correlation (new)

Also enhances the PDF report with HTC resistance breakdown table
and iteration history, and adds 55 tests covering all three modes.

commit.message:
feat: admin QMap — zoom = quadrant count, unified faction colors (#227)

Replace ZOOM_CELL_SIZES with ZOOM_LEVELS [3,10,25,50,100,250,1000] so
the zoom number represents quadrants visible across canvas width; cellSize
is computed dynamically as (canvasW - AXIS_MARGIN) / ZOOM_LEVELS[idx].
Default zoom set to index 4 (100 quadrants). Sub-pixel cells render as
1px dots for the 1000-quadrant galaxy overview. Unify FACTION_COLORS with
brighter palette used consistently for both map cells and bookmark labels
(colored left-border + text per faction).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(phase3): structured JSON output for MILESTONE PLAN, HA, BACKUP, DR

Replace fragile text-blob parsing for four table-heavy sections with a
typed JSON output pipeline.

## What changed

**app/models/section_outputs.py** (new)
- Pydantic models documenting the expected LLM JSON schema:
  MilestonePlanOutput / MilestonePhase, HighAvailabilityOutput,
  BackupStrategyOutput, DisasterRecoveryOutput.

**app/agents/writer.py**
- New STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_SECTIONS frozenset for the four sections.
- Passes json_output flag to both Jinja2 templates.
- Strips markdown code-fence wrappers (```json…```) from LLM response
  before returning, so doc_builder always receives clean JSON.

**app/templates/prompts/writer_system.j2**
- {% if json_output %} branch: "Output ONLY a single valid JSON object"
  (replaces the prose formatting rules for structured sections).

**app/templates/prompts/writer_user.j2**
- Replaces text-format instructions for MILESTONE PLAN, HIGH AVAILABILITY,
  BACKUP STRATEGY, DISASTER RECOVERY with exact JSON schemas.
- Closes the loop: final output instruction conditionally says
  "Output ONLY the raw JSON object" vs "Output only the section body".
- Examples note clarified: "content reference only — follow output
  format rules above, not the example format".

**app/services/doc_builder.py**
- _STRUCTURED_OUTPUT_SECTIONS + _STRUCTURED_SECTION_LABELS constants.
- New _inject_structured_section() static method: renders parsed JSON
  dict as bold-label + bullets paragraphs for all four sections.
  MILESTONE PLAN gets Phase N (bold) → name → bullets → duration line.
  HA/BACKUP/DR get sub-topic label (bold) → bullet lines.
- New _inject_labeled_content() method: tries json.loads() for
  structured sections; on success calls _inject_structured_section;
  on any parse failure logs a warning and falls back to
  _inject_formatted_blocks_after_element (graceful degradation).
- All four _inject_formatted_blocks_after_element call sites in
  _fill_section_content, _inject_after_known_section, and
  _append_section now route through _inject_labeled_content.

## Result
LLM output for these sections is now guaranteed-schema JSON → Pydantic-
documented → deterministic DOCX rendering. Text-parsing regex logic is
bypassed for the four sections; all other labeled-format sections
(ARCHITECTURE COMPONENTS, IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS, ARCHITECT REVIEW,
STATUS AND NEXT STEPS) are unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Scan 10: 10 bug fixes (silent death, data loss, crashes, corruption, perf)

- feed_from_hook: wrap pipeline in try/except — crash no longer silently kills hook
- dedup eviction: sort by session_id (chronological) not msg_count (wrong)
- _update_usefulness: handle string content (was iterating chars)

- decay(days=0): early return prevents ZeroDivisionError
- GlobalMemoryStatusEx: check return value before using result
- _detect_transcript_format: stop reading entire 55MB file for JSON validation
- pull_from_meta: deepcopy connections to prevent shared zones list corruption
- Bloom filter comment: fix 10%→5% to match actual threshold

Docs: Revise physics track plan from 12 to 14 lessons (#237)

Restructure the physics lesson plan based on pedagogical review:

- Narrow L03 (particle collisions): remove broadphase, keep focused on
  sphere-sphere/sphere-plane with O(n²) all-pairs
- Merge old L04 + L05 into L04 (rigid body state and orientation)
- Promote forces/torques to L05 so learners reach interactive rigid
  bodies one lesson sooner
- Add L06 (resting contacts and friction) to fill the gap before full
  collision detection
- Split GJK/EPA into two lessons (L08 + L09) — each algorithm is
  complex enough to warrant its own lesson
- Add L13 (stacking stability) for warm-starting, bias factors, and
  solver iteration tuning
- L14 (simulation loop) becomes the architectural capstone

Update common/physics/README.md planned API table to match.

Files changed:
- PLAN.md — Revised physics track from 12 to 14 lessons across 5 arcs
- common/physics/README.md — Updated planned API table with new lesson numbers

Add ink status TUI replacing bash ANSI dashboard

Standalone ink (React) TUI launched by ./seraph after Docker boots.
Two-panel layout: containers/services/session on left, real-time SSE
activity log on right. Keybindings centered in right panel bottom
border as [q]uit [r]estart [l]ogs. Deduplicates service status events,

section (not uptime-related). Simplifies seraph bash script to just
launch docker compose + exec into the TUI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Meta Graph API integration (Instagram + Facebook)

Prisma:
- New MetaIntegration model stores Facebook Page + linked Instagram
  Business Account with long-lived access token (60-day), expiry date,
  followers count, and per-vertical mapping.
- User.metaIntegrations back-relation added.

OAuth flow:
- GET /api/meta/connect    — builds Meta OAuth URL with base64url state
  (user email + target vertical), redirects to Facebook dialog
- GET /api/meta/callback   — exchanges code → short-lived token → 60-day
  long-lived token, fetches Facebook Pages + linked IG accounts via Graph
  API v20.0, upserts each into MetaIntegration, redirects to /settings

Data-fetching API routes:
- GET /api/meta/accounts             — list / DELETE connected pages
- GET /api/meta/instagram/overview   — profile + 30-day daily insights
- GET /api/meta/instagram/posts      — latest 20 posts with per-post
  reach, impressions, saves, video_views
- GET /api/meta/instagram/audience   — lifetime audience_gender_age,
  audience_country, audience_city breakdown
- GET /api/meta/instagram/stories    — active stories with reach,
  impressions, replies, exits

Middleware:
- /api/meta/callback excluded from auth guard (browser arrives here
  from Facebook's redirect before a session cookie exists)

Settings page — rebuilt with 3 tabs:
- Platform Integrations: Meta card shows live connected accounts with
  follower counts, token-expiry warnings, Disconnect and View data buttons;
  "Connect" button links to /api/meta/connect; setup checklist shown when
  not yet connected (env vars + redirect URI instructions)
- Vertical Accounts: unchanged account handle mapping
- Synced Data: account selector → live pull from all 4 API routes →
  profile card, posts table (likes/comments/reach/saves), stories grid,
  audience breakdown bars

Env vars required (add to Vercel):
  META_APP_ID       — from Meta Developer portal
  META_APP_SECRET   — from Meta Developer portal

commit.message:
Transpose spectrogram rendering for vertical timeline (#360) (#373)

Rotate canvas drawing so time flows top-to-bottom and frequency flows
left-to-right (low frequencies on the left, high on the right), matching
the new vertical timeline orientation from #359.

- SpectrogramTileRenderer: tiles are now width=frequencyBins, height=timeFrames
  (transposed from previous width=timeFrames, height=frequencyBins)
- RecordingBuffer: accumulates rows (time) instead of columns, with frequency
  bins mapped to columns (X axis)
- Spectrogram.tsx drawTilesFrame: draws tiles top-to-bottom with vertical
  viewport culling; canvas sized to viewport dimensions
- Spectrogram.tsx drawRecordingFrame: transposed to vertical time axis
- PianoRollRenderer: notes are vertical bars (x=frequency, y=time) with
  gradient and highlight adjusted for new orientation
- Removed unused height prop from Spectrogram and useContainerHeight from
  Timeline (canvas dimensions now derived from scroll container viewport)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: remove legacy src and build directories from third_party

Removed historical src/ and build/ directories that are no longer used
by the vcpkg build system. These directories were remnants from the
previous b.bat build approach.

Changes:
- Removed src/ directories: zlib, lua, librtmp
- Removed build/ directories: brotli, cares, curl, fdk-aac, getopt,
  jsoncpp, mfx, nghttp2, nghttp3, ngtcp2, opus, xxtea, zlib
- Added .gitignore to exclude output/ directories and build artifacts
- Preserved xxtea-c/ directory (still used by custom vcpkg port)

Rationale:
- vcpkg manages source code in vcpkg/buildtrees/<lib>/src/
- vcpkg manages build directories in vcpkg/buildtrees/<lib>/<triplet>-dbg/rel/
- Only third_party/<lib>/windows/output/ is needed as the final output interface
- Reduces repository size by ~1163 files

The vcpkg build scripts (build_beenet_vcpkg.bat and third_party_vcpkg/build.bat)
do not depend on these directories and continue to work without them.
